Ad Text:
Company Description

Our collaborative culture sets us apart. We help experts become true specialists and provide the long-term structure and platform to accelerate their success.

Job Description

Colliers is seeking an experienced technology platforms professional to manage and deliver the applications, systems and reporting data, and analytics solutions that support our Occupier Services Clients across Australia.

The position is centred on delivering reliable, scalable technology enablement for Occupier Clients - combining multi-client dashboard oversight with stewardship of Integrated Workplace Management Solution (IWMS) platforms that underpin Portfolio Strategy, Lease Administration, Accounts Payable and Transaction Management services.

You will work closely with our Enterprise Clients teams, business stakeholders, and Colliers technology functions to manage and enhance our technology offerings, ensuring they are operationally sound and effectively delivered.

Your responsibilities will include:

Own and manage core IWMS platforms enabling portfolio management.
Lead technology projects, including system upgrades, and new platform rollouts.
Manage day‑to‑day performance of key lease, portfolio, and transaction systems.
Manage BI and analytics outputs, including dashboards, reporting, and hands‑on data work using Excel, SQL, and BI tools.
Support business development by providing platform expertise, demonstrations, and input into RFPs and proposals.
Drive solution design, client onboarding, system adoption, training, and ongoing technology support.
Qualifications
5 – 10 years’ experience in technology platforms, applications support, systems delivery roles.
Demonstrated experience administering enterprise platforms (IWMS, ERP, or similar).
Bachelor’s degree in IT, Information Systems, Business, or a related discipline.
Experience in real estate, property, or professional services preferred.
Demonstrated experience supporting client-facing teams and commercial processes.

Technical Capability

Advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
Strong working knowledge of SQL and relational databases.
Solid understanding of the Microsoft ecosystem (Microsoft 365, Power BI; Azure concepts desirable).

What’s in It for you?

Named Forbes World's Best Employers 2025 & TIME World's Best Companies 2025!
Wide range of benefits designed to support your career, wellbeing, and lifestyle.
Industry leading leave – including 26 weeks of parental leave, additional paid leave, volunteering, and loyalty leave.
Formal career development & recognition programs.
Innovative technology & tools to drive your productivity.
Additional Information

We foster an environment in which everyone at Colliers feels respected and comfortable bringing their authentic selves to do their best work. It’s our people who set us apart and we actively recruit people who represent the diversity of the communities in which we operate. We take great pride in doing what’s right and operating in ways that benefit the business, our people, our clients, our communities, and the environment.

Experts join experts, and we welcome you to join us as we lead the industry into the future.

Please be advised that applications will only be accepted directly rather than via recruitment agencies.

#LI-JB1

$125 to $130 per hour (inc Super)
Hybrid Working Arrangements - Melbourne, Sydney, Canberra, Brisbane
12-month Contract
NV1 Clearance is Mandatory
About INDEX Consultants
INDEX Consultants delivers technology solutions to drive business success. We are part of a $8B Tokyo-based global group, with Australian offices in Melbourne, Sydney, Canberra and Brisbane.
We take great pride in being trusted advisors to the Public, Financial Services, Energy & Resources, Health and Education Sectors.
Our consultants are highly experienced, boasting an average of 15 years' industry experience across a wide range of subject matter expertise in complex technical and business projects.
We're passionate about what we do and are want passionate people to join our rapidly growing team.

About the Client
Our client is a large, mission‑critical public sector organisation responsible for delivering essential services to millions of Australians. They are undertaking a major transformation program to modernise core systems, uplift data capability and improve service delivery through citizen‑centric, digital platforms. This program plays a vital role in maintaining integrity, transparency and trust in nationally significant processes.

About the Role
This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced Data Modeller to join a large-scale transformation program focused on modernising enterprise data platforms. You will work across complex, high‑impact systems, designing robust data models that enable improved analytics, governance and decision‑making. The role offers exposure to modern cloud technologies, Agile delivery and senior stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities
Design and implement conceptual, logical and physical data models across transactional, analytical and big‑data platforms
Translate complex business requirements into scalable, long‑term data solutions
Evaluate and optimise existing data systems to improve quality, performance and interoperability
Collaborate with Agile delivery teams and stakeholders to develop data flows and analytical solutions
Support data governance, metadata management and continuous improvement initiatives
Skills Required
Minimum 3 years' hands‑on experience in data modelling using best‑practice techniques and tools
Strong expertise in data warehousing, data lakes and enterprise big‑data solutions, including cloud platforms
Expert knowledge of metadata management, data quality and governance frameworks
Excellent problem‑solving, communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with experience leading or mentoring teams
Proven ability to produce high‑quality technical documentation using appropriate standards and version control
You can be based in Melbourne, Sydney, Brisbane or Canberra
Australian Citizens with an NV1 Clearance

INDEX Consultants value equal opportunity and advocate for diversity and inclusion. We welcome and encourage applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of race, gender, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or age to apply. So even if you don't tick all the boxes, we would still like to hear from you. Please let us know if you require any reasonable adjustments during the interview process.

Make a difference everyday

Join HammondCare and help improve quality of life for older Australians in need. As a not-for-profit, Christian organisation, we provide expert aged care, home care, dementia care and palliative care. Mission-led, we build passionate teams who support older people through life's most difficult circumstances.

With over 90 years of experience, we're known for our relationship-based approach. Whether it's in our homes, hospitals or the community, we bring skill, empathy and innovation to every moment of care.

It's not just care. It's HammondCare.

About the role

The role of Senior BI Developer is responsible for designing, developing and supporting on premises and cloud based system integrations across HammondCare's application and data landscape.


This is a permanent full-time role based at St Leonards with hybrid opportunities.

What we can offer you

We care about your wellbeing at HammondCare and as a result offer a wide range of health and lifestyle benefits including:

NFP salary packaging ($15,900 tax free) plus meals and entertainment benefit (up to $2,650 tax free)
Additional leave purchase options and 14 weeks paid parental leave
Flexible working options, including hybrid arrangements
Discounts on everyday products and services
Discounted healthcare memberships - access to network of fitness centres and pools for employees and families
Free access to independent counselling support for employees and families
Health & Wellbeing programmes
Reward & Recognition programmes
Referral bonuses
Career and professional development opportunities

Key Responsibilities include

Integration Design & Development

Design, develop, and maintain on-premises, cloud, and hybrid integrations across enterprise systems
Build and maintain integrations using a mix of: On Prem integration tools (e.g. SSIS), Microsoft Fabric components (e.g. Lakehouse, Data Pipelines, Notebooks) Cloud native services (e.g. Azure Data Factory, APIs) iPaaS platforms (e.g. Workato) for SaaS and event driven integrations
Develop API based, file based, database, and event driven integrations as required.
Ensure integration solutions align with enterprise architecture, security, and data governance standards

Data Quality, Reliability & Performance

Monitor and support integration workloads across on Prem and cloud environments
Implement robust error handling, logging, alerting, and retry mechanisms across all integration platforms
Troubleshoot and resolve issues spanning network boundaries, authentication models, and system dependencies

Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement

Work closely with application owners, vendors, and business stakeholders to design fit for purpose integration solutions
Support integration between internal systems and third party SaaS platforms using various tools
Collaborate with business and Infrastructure teams to support end to end data flows

DevOps, Governance & Documentation

Use version control and CI/CD practices to manage integration assets across on prem and cloud platforms
Document integration architectures, data mappings, schedules, dependencies, and operational support procedures
Ensure integrations comply with security, privacy, and regulatory requirements

About you

Commitment to engage and align with HammondCare's Christian Mission, Motivation,
and Mission in Action.
Minimum 5 years' experience in a similar role in a fast paced environment
Relevant IT / Computing Science / Data Analytics / Data Science
Strong proficiency in SQL for data extraction, transformation, troubleshooting, and supporting integration and Fabric ingestion pipelines. (5+ Years)
Handson experience using Python for API integrations, data transformation, automation, validation, and bespoke processing, including use within Fabric notebooks where applicable. (5+ Years)
Practical experience with SSIS (on prem) and ADF (cloud) to build and support hybrid integration workflows. (3+ Years)
Experience using Workato (or similar iPaaS tools) to deliver SaaS, event driven, and work flow based integrations. (3+ Years)
Experience ingesting and preparing data in Microsoft Fabric (e.g. Lakehouse, pipelines/notebooks) to support Power BI and enterprise analytics. (3+ Years)
Post graduate qualifications in management and business administration or technology discipline (desirable)
Professional certifications in areas of project management, data architecture, ITIL, analytics or related accreditations demonstrating a commitment to lifelong learning (desirable)

To Apply

If you are ready to make a difference with HammondCare, Apply Now! For any specific queries please contact talent@hammond.com.au.

Pre- Employment Checks

All short-listed applicants for these positions will be asked to consent to a criminal record check and reference checking.

HammondCare is committed to creating an inclusive workplace that promotes and values diversity and inclusion. We strive on creating an equal employment environment where everyone from any background can be themselves.

This is a founding Data hire, a Lead Data/Analytics Engineer within a fast-growing, venture-backed SaaS company solving complex problems to make the world a better place! You will take full ownership from a hands on perspective in building the data function and platform from the ground up in a true greenfield environment.

The business already generates large volumes of data across multiple systems, but it is currently unstructured creating a high-impact opportunity to design a scalable, well-modelled data foundation. This is a hands-on individual contributor role with significant influence, working closely with leadership to shape how data is used across the organisation.

This position won't suit someone who has worked in large corporates for the entirety of their career - you should have experience in start up/scale up (unstructured) environments, you will be expected to lay the foundations for growth while working closely with the Co-Founder & Chief Product Officer.

Responsibilities
Design and build the end-to-end data platform from scratch
Develop and maintain scalable data pipelines and ETL processes
Create clean, well-structured data models and curated data layers
Translate complex business workflows into usable data structures
Partner with stakeholders to define data requirements and use cases
Establish best practices across data architecture, modelling, and governance
Enable analytics across product, growth, and operational functions
Consolidate and unify data from multiple sources into a single platform
Requirements
5+ years experience in Data Engineering or Analytics Engineering roles
Strong expertise in data modelling and dbt
Hands-on experience setting up greenfield Databricks (or similar) modern data platforms
Experience building data systems in greenfield or early-stage environments
Strong SQL and data warehousing fundamentals
Ability to work with ambiguity and translate business needs into technical solutions
Strong stakeholder engagement skills and commercial awareness
Experience with cloud platforms AWS/GCP, BigQuery etc..
What This Role Will Do for Your Career
Give you full ownership of a data function from day one
Provide the opportunity to design and implement a modern data stack end-to-end
Develop deep expertise in solving complex, real-world data problems
Position you as a senior technical leader without needing to manage a team
Offer exposure to high-growth scaling challenges in a fast-moving environment
Enable close collaboration with leadership on business-critical decisions

Please note**

- This position requires Sydney based candidates, willing to work 3 days on-site in Sydney CBD

- You must be Australian Permanent Resident or Citizen

- Only those who meet the above criteria will be considered

Our purpose

Here at Datacom, we connect people and technology in order to solve challenges, create opportunities and discover new possibilities for the communities we live in.

About the Role

We are seeking a Senior Technical Business Analyst with very strong API and integration experience to join our high-performing, agile teams. This is a hands-on, technically-focused BA role in a multinational environment, working with diverse clients, high-paced delivery, and modern engineering practices.

You will operate as the key bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams, with a strong emphasis on API-driven solutions, integration design, and scalable, secure services. You’ll work closely with architects, developers, and testers to ensure that business and technical requirements are clearly defined, well-documented, and successfully delivered.

If you enjoy working on complex integration initiatives, API ecosystems and cloud-native platforms, and you want to extend your impact across new clients, teams and technologies, this role is for you.

What you’ll do:

Requirements & Analysis

Understand client business domains, existing processes, systems and integration landscapes.
Lead requirements discovery and analysis for API and integration-focused projects.
Elicit and document functional and non-functional requirements (including performance, security, resilience).
Translate business requirements into detailed technical specifications for APIs, integrations and data flows.
Create and maintain:
Jira tickets / user stories with clear, testable acceptance criteria.
API and integration requirements, mapping documents and interface specifications.
UML/sequence diagrams and integration design patterns.
Mapping tables, data contracts and field-level mappings between consumers and providers.
Environment and endpoint documentation, Postman collections, and related assets in Confluence.

API & Integration Focus

Lead the analysis and design of REST and SOAP integrations, with a strong bias toward modern RESTful APIs.
Design and maintain API contracts (Swagger/OpenAPI) and govern changes across consumers and providers.
Work with teams using:
Spring Boot and Java-based microservices 
Netflix OSS stack (Zuul, Ribbon, Eureka) or similar service discovery and gateway solutions.
Help define API standards, best practices and reuse patterns across the platform.
Contribute to API security, error handling, resilience, and observability requirements.
Collaborate on messaging and streaming patterns (e.g. ActiveMQ, Kafka and similar event/queue-based systems).
Support performance, load and integration testing (e.g. Rest Assured, Gatling, Hoverfly) by ensuring test scenarios align with business and technical requirements.

Delivery & Collaboration

Work in an agile (Scrum/SaFe) environment and actively participate in:
Backlog refinement, sprint planning, and reviews.
3 Amigos sessions (Developer, Tester, BA) to clarify requirements and acceptance criteria.
Workshops and stakeholder sessions for requirements, design and prioritisation.
Collaborate closely with:
Technical Leads and Architects to ensure solution and integration architecture align with business needs.
Developers to resolve specification queries and clarify integration behaviour.
Test Analysts to ensure coverage of functional and non-functional requirements, and awareness of changes, issues, and risks.
Provide effort estimates for change requests and larger pieces of work using a clear, traceable estimation approach.
Contribute to and/or lead the production, review and signoff of:
Requirements documentation.
Functional and integration design documents.
User guides and operational handover materials as needed.
Ensure all required project reviews and checkpoints are completed to a high standard.

Technical & Conceptual Skills

You bring strong, hands-on experience and understanding across:

Toolkit / Tools

Confluence & Jira.
Swagger / OpenAPI (API contract design and maintenance).
Postman, Rest Assured, SOAP UI for API and integration testing.
Spring Boot, Java 11+/17+, Gradle (awareness of how services are built and deployed).
Netflix Stack (Zuul, Ribbon, Eureka) or equivalent discovery/gateway components.
Splunk or similar logging/observability tools.
Git/Bitbucket, IntelliJ/Eclipse or similar IDEs.
Additional testing and simulation tools (e.g. Gatling, Hoverfly) desirable.

Concepts

REST APIs and modern API design principles.
API security (authentication, authorisation, OAuth2/OpenID Connect, tokens).
API best practices and APIs at scale (versioning, governance, rate limiting, backwards compatibility).
Reusability and modular service design.
Integration patterns and flows (sync/async, messaging, pub/sub, request–reply).
Error handling and resilience patterns.
Service discovery and microservices architecture.
Containerisation and cloud concepts (deployment, environments, scalability).
Distributed tracing, logging, monitoring and observability.
CI/CD pipeline awareness and how it impacts requirements and releases.
Agile and SAFe concepts and ceremonies.
ITSM practices around change and release (awareness).

Professional Qualities

5+ years’ experience as a Technical Business Analyst in agile teams, with a strong emphasis on APIs and integrations.
Proven track record working closely with development and architecture teams on API-driven solutions.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with high attention to detail.
Comfortable working in fast-paced, multi-team, multi-stakeholder environments.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to translate between business and technical audiences.
Deadline-driven, proactive, and able to own outcomes from discovery through to delivery.
Collaborative, team-oriented, and confident facilitating workshops and discussions.
Proficient in English.

About Datacom

We have over 6,200 people across our global offices, and we have extensive expertise in operating data centres, providing IT services, software engineering and application management, as well as payroll and customer service design and operations. With this comes a long list of significant clients Datacom is committed to hiring, developing and promoting the best talent from a diverse range of backgrounds. We are local at heart, yet world-class in capability.

About QIC

As one of Australias leading institutional investment managers with more than $100 billion in assets under management, we have a presence across four continents, seven offices and assets across global markets.

At QIC, we believe in shared value - where delivering strong investment returns can go hand in hand with doing good for our clients, people and communities. If we invest well, we will all do better together.

Life at QIC

We know that our people perform at their best when they feel a sense of belonging and are encouraged to bring their authentic selves to work every day.  We provide a supportive work environment where everyone can thrive and feel valued for their meaningful contribution in delivering for the prosperity of our clients, people and communities.

Joining QIC, you can contribute to our employee led networks that champion our diversity, equity and inclusion strategy, which consists of five key pillars: Gender Equality, LGBTQ+, Accessibility, Cultural & Linguistic Diversity, and First Nations People.

Other reasons to join us:
Flexibility and hybrid options encouraged – certified Family Inclusive Workplace
Recharge leave - additional 3 days leave per year
Annual Fitness/home office reimbursement
Ability to swap public holidays for those of personal, cultural or religious significance
26 weeks gender-neutral paid parental leave
Financial study assistance and study leave
Community leave for supporting the causes you love
Development opportunities to extend, expand and enhance your growth.
At QIC, we welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, the LGBTQI+ community, people with disabilities and people from all cultural backgrounds. We have an equitable, barrier-free application process so please inform us of your accessibility requirements in your application or you can reach out to our Talent Team on 07 3360 3800.

About the role

This is not your average trading role. At QIC, you’ll be part of a 24-hour global execution platform, working across asset classes including equities, fixed interest, commodities, FX, and derivatives. You’ll execute trades with precision, contribute to execution research, and help drive innovation in trading processes.

This role reports to the Senior Manager, Trading – Liquid Markets Group and works closely with the Head of Portfolio Implementation. You’ll be well-supported by experienced leaders who are passionate about collaboration and delivering great outcomes for clients.

You’ll work closely with Portfolio Managers, Risk & Compliance, and external counterparties to ensure best execution and deliver value to our clients. This is a hands-on, fast-paced role where every day is different and every trade counts.

The team is known for its close-knit, collaborative culture where staff longevity speaks volumes, and many team members have built long-term careers within the group. It’s a supportive environment that fosters professional development through shared success, mutual respect, and a genuine enjoyment of working together.

Key responsibilities:

Execute trades across multiple asset classes with precision and compliance.

Contribute to transaction cost analysis and execution research.

Build and maintain strong relationships with counterparties and internal teams.

Support data management and reporting tools for analytics and client reporting.

Monitor market intelligence and regulatory developments.

Assist with trade preparation, authorisation, and audit activities.

Drive innovation and process improvement across trading operations.

About you

You’re curious, analytical, and thrive in a dynamic environment. You bring institutional trading desk experience and a strong grasp of database management and analytics. You’re collaborative, detail-oriented, and not afraid to roll up your sleeves.

To succeed in this role, you will have:

Experience in institutional trading, ideally across global markets including options.

Strong analytical and technical skills, including database management.

A collaborative mindset and excellent communication skills.

Ability to support offshore trading hours on a rotational basis.

A proactive attitude and high attention to detail.

Relevant tertiary qualifications (finance, commerce, engineering, etc.).

Prior experience trading equity options and familiarity with platforms such as Bloomberg, Tradeweb, or BlackRock Solutions and SQL and Python coding skills will be highly regarded. QIC is a place where careers grow, ideas thrive, and people stick around. With a culture built on respect, challenge, and fun, QIC offers more than just a job—it’s a place to build your future.

Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you'd like, where you'll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you'll be able to reimagine what's possible. Job description:

Your role

We are seeking an experienced SAP Analytics Lead to drive analytics strategy, design, and delivery across SAP and enterprise data platforms. This role will work closely with business stakeholders, enterprise architects, and delivery teams to convert business requirements into scalable, insight driven analytics solutions.

Key Responsibilities

Own and drive the SAP analytics vision and roadmap aligned to business priorities.
Act as the Analytics Lead for SAP programs, ensuring architecture, design, and delivery quality.
Partner with business leaders to translate reporting, planning, and analytics needs into actionable solutions.
Lead and mentor analytics teams (onshore/offshore), fostering best practices and delivery excellence.
Contribute to thought leadership, pre sales activities, and client roadmap discussions.
Design and govern end to end analytics architectures using:
o SAP Analytics Cloud (Reporting, Planning, Predictive)
o SAP Datasphere (Business Data Cloud / BDC)
o Define data models, semantic layers, and data flows from SAP S/4HANA and non SAP sources.
Ensure alignment with enterprise data, security, and governance standards.
Guide performance optimisation, scalability, and future ready design.
Lead requirements workshops, solution design sessions, and design authority reviews.
Oversee build, testing, deployment, and hypercare activities.
Ensure high quality deliverables, on time milestones, and effective risk management.
Support UAT, business sign off, and smooth transition to operations.
Act as the primary analytics point of contact for senior client stakeholders.
Provide clear status, risks, and mitigation plans to program leadership.
Collaborate with SAP functional, integration, and security teams.



Profile description:

Your profile
Strong hands on and leadership experience with:
SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) - stories, planning models, live & acquired connectivity
SAP BW/4HANA
SAP Datasphere (BDC)
Experience integrating SAP and non SAP data sources.
Solid understanding of data modelling concepts (star schemas, analytical views, semantic layers).
Knowledge of SAP S/4HANA data structures and analytics enablement.
10+ years in analytics / BI with significant SAP focus.
Proven experience leading teams and large scale SAP analytics programs.
Ability to influence architecture decisions and guide executives.
Experience in consulting or complex enterprise client environments is highly desirable.

Desirable Experience

SAP Certified in SAC, BDC, or Datasphere.
Experience with planning and forecasting use cases in SAC.
Exposure to cloud data platforms (Azure, AWS, GCP) and advanced analytics.
Pre sales, proposal development, or solution estimation experience.


We offer:



What you'll love about working here



You will be a part of a diverse collective of free-thinkers, entrepreneurs and industry experts. You will love the exposure to the scale of transformation, the depth of expertise, and the opportunities for growth.
We aim to build an environment where employees can enjoy a positive work-life balance. We embed hybrid working in all that we do and make flexible working arrangements the day-to-day reality for our people.
At the heart of our mission is your career growth. You will have countless learning and development opportunities from thinktanks to hackathons, and access to 250,000 courses with numerous external certifications crafted to support you in exploring a world of opportunities.
We realise a Total Reward package should be more than just compensation. We offer a range of core and flexible benefits and have a Peer Recognition Portal called ‘Celebrate'.
We don't just believe in inclusion; we actively go out to making it a working reality. Driven by our core values and ‘Inclusive future for all' campaign, we build environments where you can bring your whole self to work.

Apply today!

We’re seeking a highly experienced Technical Business Analyst across Wellington, Auckland, Melbourne, and Sydney to join a large‑scale, multi‑year transformation program modernising customer communications across enterprise platforms. This strategic initiative impacts millions of customer interactions every year, with a committed roadmap that offers long‑term engagement, ongoing innovation, and the chance to work on highly complex, meaningful problems.

With strong SmartCOMM expertise, you’ll play a critical role in bridging business needs and technical solutions—working closely with clients, developers, and QA teams to shape and deliver SmartCOMM‑based customer communication solutions end‑to‑end. From analysing complex integration scenarios to defining functional and technical specifications, you’ll be hands‑on throughout the delivery lifecycle. You’ll thrive in a dynamic, client‑facing environment, collaborating with a highly energetic team and contributing to Agile ways of working while delivering solutions that genuinely improve customer communication at scale.

This role sits within our Information Services practice, one of four key areas at Tenzing, alongside Technology Services, Management Consulting, and Managed Services. The Information Services team partners with clients to deliver all things data including data science, data warehousing, and data migrations. We use proven methodologies along with industry-leading technology (like Snowflake!) to create scalable and sustainable data solutions tailored to our customer’s needs.

What you bring

You'll have proven ability in:

Business analysis in technical environments (at least 5 years' experience)

Working with SmartComm or similar CCM platforms

SmartComm qualification and practical delivery experience is a must!

Gathering and documenting business and technical requirements

Designing and validating customer communication workflows and document templates

Collaborating with developers and QA teams to ensure successful delivery

Understanding of REST APIs and integration patterns

Agile methodologies and facilitation skills

It’s important you have a minimum of three years’ experience in a consultancy, a strong background in application QA or development, and the ability to work independently. You’ll also be skilled in documentation, confident in estimating testing effort, and delivering work in line with those estimates.


Why join Tenzing, who are we?

At Tenzing, we’ve been helping clients scale their mountains for over twenty years. We call it the Sherpa ethos - standing alongside our clients, doing the heavy lifting, and guiding them through complex transformations. As trusted partners in change, we work with some of Australasia’s leading organisations to deliver strategic and operational impact across industries.
Tenzing is an independent subsidiary of the Tech Mahindra Group, giving our people access to a global network of expertise and opportunities, while maintaining our well-known Tenzing ways of working and deep understanding of the local context across New Zealand and Australia.

We believe our people are our greatest asset. That’s why we invest in tailored career development, mentorship, and a collaborative environment where creativity and professionalism thrive. We also take the time to celebrate our wins, recognising individual and team achievements, and fostering a culture of appreciation and shared success.
Our leadership team is made up of highly experienced professionals who are passionate about what they do. They are deeply knowledgeable, approachable, and committed to empowering their teams, encouraging individuals to take on new challenges, grow their capabilities, and make a meaningful impact. Come meet them!

What we offer:

Competitive Salary

Discretionary annual bonus

Flexible and hybrid working options.

Ability to purchase additional annual leave.

Life and Income Protection Insurance (fully subsidised)

Discounted Medical Insurance

Comprehensive Learning & Development programmes

Wellbeing support including EAP and flu vaccinations

Inclusive culture with regular social events and celebrations

If you're ready to work on meaningful projects, expand your technical toolkit, and grow within a consulting environment, we’d love to hear from you.

Job Title: Clinical Coding Specialist - Health Information Manager
Ad Text:
About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Clinical Coding Specialist / Health Information Manager to join our Code Focus team and play a key role in shaping how clinical coding expertise is embedded into systems used by hospitals across Australia.

In this role, you will contribute to the ongoing evolution and optimisation of Code Focus and its associated modules. You will apply your clinical coding expertise to ensure system guidance, rules, and prompts remain current, practical, and aligned with national standards and industry advice, supporting efficient coding workflows and strong funding outcomes for health services.

This role is well suited to someone who enjoys working at the intersection of health information and systems and is comfortable using digital platforms to embed subject matter expertise into practical, system-driven solutions. You will collaborate closely with clients, internal product and development teams, and business stakeholders, providing informed guidance and contributing to considered product and enhancement decisions.

Key Responsibilities

Provide clinical coding subject matter expertise to support the ongoing development and refinement of Code Focus features, including system guidance, notifications, and prompts

Review and incorporate updates from ICD-10-AM editions, ACS, ACHI, and relevant industry advice into the platform

Coordinate and synthesise clinical coding guidance from recognised industry sources for inclusion within Code Focus

Engage with existing clients to understand requirements, triage requests, and support prioritisation of enhancements and incidents

Support the implementation of Code Focus at new health services, including configuration, validation, and advisory support

Assist with coordination and prioritisation of service desk enquiries

Work with the Business Development Manager to articulate product value, points of difference, and business outcomes to prospective clients

Act as an internal Application Specialist and Subject Matter Expert across clinical coding, casemix, and funding models

Required Skills and Experience

Minimum 5 years’ clinical coding experience, ideally including time as a Coding Educator and/or Auditor

Strong working knowledge of ICD-10-AM, ACHI, and ACS, including completion of Thirteenth Edition education

Experience working with health information systems, including exposure to system administration, configuration, implementation, or enhancement activities within hospital or healthcare environments

Sound understanding of hospital workflows, Health Information Systems, and coding operations

Demonstrated knowledge of activity-based funding and casemix principles

Clear, professional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present information and provide considered advice to a range of stakeholders

An understanding of the software development lifecycle, with the ability to work effectively with product and development teams to translate clinical and business requirements into system outcomes

Strong self-management skills and the ability to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team

Qualifications

Clinical Coding qualification, Bachelor of Health Information Management, or equivalent professional experience

Desirable Experience

Experience coding across both public and private health services

Exposure to interstate funding models, including National Weighted Activity Units (NWAU)

Understanding of alternative funding models such as ANSNAP and mental health

Foundation knowledge of health system integration concepts, including HL7 messaging

Familiarity with emerging AI-enabled clinical coding workflows

Project or implementation experience within health information or digital health environments.

About Data Agility

Data Agility is a specialist analytics and information management firm delivering AI and analytics solutions alongside data governance, quality, migration, integration, reporting, and the strategies that bring these capabilities together. Since 2003, we have partnered with organisations to apply trusted data in practical ways to address business challenges and improve performance.

Our specialist Health Practice has delivered over 300 projects nationally and is recognised for its comprehensive understanding of health sector ICT environments, including the complexities of system implementation, integration, ongoing operation, and support.

About Code Focus

Code Focus is Data Agility’s flagship product, and the leading Australian end-to-end HIM coding lifecycle and workflow automation platform created by Health Information Managers for Health Information Managers.

Used by public and private health services across Australia, Code Focus supports the complete clinical coding lifecycle, enabling health services to accurately capture CDI activity and impact, optimise funding outcomes, and make informed, data-driven operational and financial decisions. Through intelligent automation, Code Focus improves efficiency across HIM teams, with each implementation configured to suit the health service's specific needs using AI-powered Encoder and Grouper, Workflow Management, Real Time Coder Feedback and Validation, Auditing and Clinical Documentation Improvement (CDI) solutions, underpinned by more than 20 years of health information expertise.

Why Join Us

This role offers the opportunity to work with a well-established product used by health services across Australia, alongside a highly experienced team with deep sector knowledge. You will have meaningful influence over how clinical coding guidance is embedded into technology, supporting better outcomes for both coders and health services.

LIFECYCLE & RETENTION MARKETING MANAGER

THE ROLE:

Bared Footwear presently operates in six markets (AU, NZ, UK, SG, HK, US) and is building a full-funnel growth ecosystem under a newly created Head of Growth function. This role owns the customer lifecycle from first contact through repeat purchase and advocacy, with the goal of turning one-time buyers into high-LTV repeat customers.

This is not a campaign scheduling role. Yes, you will build and optimise flows and campaigns daily. But your mandate is broader: establish our CDP capability, design the segmentation and data architecture that powers personalisation at scale, and create the feedback loop between retention and acquisition that makes every marketing dollar work harder. You will think in terms of CLV, repeat rate, ERFM and owned channel contribution, not just deliverability and click-throughs.

You will work in lockstep with the Demand & Acquisition Manager and closely with Marketing, eComm, Tech, Ops, Retail and Customer Service teams to ensure customer data is accurate, actionable and consistently leveraged across all channels.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Lifecycle strategy and execution

Own the end-to-end lifecycle roadmap across email, SMS and emerging channels (push, WhatsApp) for all markets.

Design, build and optimise automated flows in Klaviyo: welcome, post-purchase, browse/cart abandonment, winback, VIP/loyalty, sunset, cross-sell, upsell, etc.

Evolve toward behaviour-triggered, personalised journeys informed by purchase history, intent and predicted CLV.

Partner with Marketing on EDM strategy, segmentation, testing plans and list builds. Act as the final quality checkpoint for all CRM-driven sends.


Customer data and governance

Evaluate, select and activate the right solution to unify customer profiles, configure identity resolution and establish a single source of truth across all storefronts (CDP or composable via Data Warehouse).

Implement RFM analysis, predictive CLV modelling, funnel analysis and product analytics.

Build and maintain advanced segmentation using behavioural, transactional and predictive attributes. Define and maintain Ideal Customer Profiles (ICPs).

Establish data governance standards: naming conventions, event taxonomy, profile hygiene and consent management across six markets.

Oversee list quality, govern acquisition sources (forms, zero party collection, campaigns) and manage onsite capture points.

Stay informed on global privacy regulations (GDPR, Australian Privacy Act, PDPA etc.) and ensure CRM/CDP/Data Warehouse systems strictly honour consent requirements.

Loyalty and retention

Support the development and optimisation of loyalty marketing and retention initiatives.

Use ERFM insights to identify opportunities to improve repeat purchase rate, engagement and lifetime value.

Ensure loyalty segments and behaviours are accurately captured and accessible across systems. Track programme performance and recommend enhancements.


Growth ecosystem integration

Build suppression and exclusion audiences to improve paid media efficiency and reduce wasted spend on existing customers.

Create high-value seed audiences from lifecycle segments for lookalike targeting across paid channels.

Work in lockstep with the Demand & Acquisition Manager to ensure retention data feeds acquisition strategy: suppression, seed audiences and customer intelligence that makes every paid dollar work harder.

Drive measurable incremental revenue from owned channels, contributing to MER and EBITDA improvement.

Report on lifecycle contribution to growth KPIs: owned channel revenue, repeat purchase rate, LTV:CAC ratio and net revenue per customer.


Testing, reporting and innovation

Design and execute A/B and multivariate tests across subject lines, send times, flow logic, personalisation and channel mix.

Build and maintain lifecycle performance dashboards. Analyse customer behaviour via Klaviyo, Shopify and attribution tools.

Conduct regular flow audits and campaign post-mortems. Present insights and recommendations to the Growth team, broader stakeholders and senior leaders.

Stay across emerging AI, automation and personalisation trends. Identify opportunities to improve CRM workflow efficiency and evaluate new tools or integrations.

REQUIREMENTS & PERSONAL ATTRIBUTES:

6+ years in a CRM, lifecycle, retention or customer data role within an ecommerce environment.

Advanced Klaviyo expertise: complex flows, Liquid scripting, conditional splits, suppression logic and advanced segmentation.

Shopify Plus operational knowledge.

Strong analytical skills, comfortable navigating tools like Klaviyo reporting, Shopify analytics and GA4.

Deep understanding of lifecycle marketing, segmentation and automation best practices.

Experience managing customer data with a focus on hygiene and system integrity.

Understanding of CDP concepts: identity resolution, data unification and audience activation.

Familiarity with RFM segmentation, predictive CLV or funnel analysis.

Hands-on experience with a CDP.

Multi-market or multi-storefront experience.

Experience in fashion, retail or omnichannel businesses.

Looker Studio, BigQuery or other BI tool proficiency.

Experience building paid media suppression or seed audiences from lifecycle segments.

Familiarity with TripleWhale or similar attribution tools.

Exposure to AI-powered CRM tools or autonomous optimisation platforms.

Endlessly curious and motivated to stay ahead of CRM, data and technology trends.

Data-driven but not data-paralysed. Can make decisions with imperfect information and iterate.

Clear communicator who can translate performance data into business language for senior stakeholders.

Collaborative and ego-free. Understands that retention and acquisition are two halves of the same engine.

Customer-centric, with a genuine interest in understanding behaviour that drives value.

Thinks about lifecycle as a growth lever, not just a channel.

Genuinely passionate about fashion, design and brand. You notice the details that others scroll past.


THE BENEFITS:

A newly created Growth function with the mandate to build from the ground up. This role will define what demand and acquisition looks like at Bared for years to come.

A supportive and inclusive work environment.

Be part of a company dedicated to making a positive impact, and a proud B Corp certified company.

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts.

Ad Text:
Join a high-performing lending team where your expertise will make a real impact.

An established and growing business in the mortgage and property finance sector is looking for a Senior Credit Analyst to take the lead on complex lending scenarios and help drive strong approval outcomes across a busy, high-quality portfolio.

If you’re someone who knows credit inside out, enjoys solving complex deals, and takes pride in getting applications right the first time, this is the kind of role where you’ll be trusted, valued, and challenged.

As the Senior Credit Analyst, you’ll play a critical role in assessing, structuring, and progressing loan applications from submission through to approval. You’ll work closely with advisers and lenders to ensure deals are positioned effectively and managed with precision.

What you’ll bring
Proven experience in a Credit Analyst or Senior Credit Analyst role, working on complex residential loan applications.
Strong background in mortgage broking, banking, or non-bank lending
Excellent knowledge of residential lending policy, servicing, and credit assessment
Confidence managing complex scenarios, including self-employed applicants and investors

Ideal for someone who

Our client is a rapidly growing, multi-site organisation operating nationally and undergoing a significant period of transformation under a newly appointed CFO.

This newly created FP&A Lead position reports directly to the CFO and will play a pivotal role in supporting the organisation through its next stage of growth.
At its core, the role is responsible for turning financial and operational data into clear, actionable insights that drive decision-making across the business. With a complex multi-site model, a key focus will be establishing a single source of truth across performance, forecasting, and commercial reporting.

Key responsibilities include:

Lead the annual budgeting process and maintain a rolling forecast aligned to key operational drivers

Support the transition from a heavily Excel-based environment to a more scalable FP&A framework

Contribute to the selection and implementation of a best-in-class budgeting and forecasting tool
Play a key role in building out the FP&A function as the business scales.
Initially, this role will be hands-on, requiring someone comfortable working in the detail while also bringing a clear view of best practice.

About You
You are a commercially minded FP&A professional who thrives in dynamic, high-growth environments. You are an established Finance Business Partner with a strong strategic outlook. 
You will bring:

Experience within a large, complex, multi-site organisation (e.g. retail, FMCG, healthcare, aged care)
Strong commercial acumen and the ability to influence senior stakeholders
Advanced analytical capability and strong attention to detail

CA/CPA qualified with a minimum of five years commercial finance experience.

Ad Text:
Senior Change Analyst

Contract to 30/11/2026 | Brisbane CBD | Hybrid working

We are partnering with a leading Queensland Government client to recruit an experienced Senior Change Analyst. In this role, you will take on a pivotal role in driving change initiatives associated with the solution migration and upgrade and supporting the impacted teams with the transition.

Key responsibilities:

Adhere to defined service quality standards, health and safety policies and procedures relating to the work being undertaken to ensure high quality, safe services and workplaces.

Mentor and participate in knowledge and skill sharing activities with other members of the team.

Collaborate with customer stakeholders to identify change and communications impacts from documented solution impacts.

About the Company

This nationally recognised services organisation employs over 9,500 people across Australia and operates within a complex, workforce-intensive environment. With a strong operational footprint and a focus on continuous improvement, the business is investing in its systems and processes to better support frontline teams and drive efficiency at scale.

With ongoing transformation across workforce platforms, there is a strong focus on improving how systems are used, integrated, and understood across the business.

Technology plays a critical role in enabling operations, and this role sits at the centre,connecting systems, people, and processes to drive better outcomes.

 

 

The Opportunity

We’re looking for a curious, hands-on Systems Analyst who enjoys getting into the detail, asking questions, and figuring out how things really work.

You’ll take ownership of workforce management systems, acting as the go-to person for support, analysis, and continuous improvement. This isn’t just about keeping things running, you’ll be encouraged to challenge, test, and improve the environment, finding smarter ways to use existing systems.

You’ll work closely with both technical teams and the business, translating requirements, troubleshooting issues, and helping teams get the most out of the platform.

This role suits someone who:

Likes to dig deep and solve problems at the root cause
Isn’t afraid to test, break, and improve systems
Can confidently engage with stakeholders across the business
Thrives in environments where things aren’t always perfectly defined



What You'll Do

In this role, you’ll be hands-on with workforce systems day-to-day, investigating issues, improving functionality, and ensuring the platform is working effectively for the business. You’ll balance problem-solving with stakeholder engagement, helping teams navigate and optimise how they use the system:

Provide hands-on support across workforce management platforms, including UKG
Investigate, troubleshoot, and resolve system issues across environments
Manage system updates, releases, and enhancements, assessing impact on the business
Work closely with vendors and internal teams to resolve complex technical issues
Analyse system performance and identify opportunities for improvement
Develop and maintain documentation, processes, and workflows
Support testing activities, including test planning and execution
Build strong relationships with business stakeholders to understand system usage and challenges
Translate technical issues into clear, practical outcomes for non-technical users



What You'll Bring

You’re naturally curious and enjoy getting under the hood of systems to understand how they really work. You’re comfortable navigating complexity, asking questions, and working closely with both technical teams and the business to find practical solutions:

3+ years’ experience in systems analysis, application support, or workforce systems
Experience with workforce management systems (UKG highly regarded)
Strong problem-solving skills with a hands-on, investigative approach
Comfortable working in a “break/fix” environment where priorities can shift
Ability to balance technical work with stakeholder communication
Strong documentation and analytical skills, including reporting
Confident working across multiple systems and integrations
A proactive mindset, with the ability to take ownership and drive outcomes

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer – Melbourne - $180,000 - Gain "productionised" AI
Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer – Melbourne - $180,000 - Gain "productionised" Agentic AI and Gen AI 


Morgan Consulting are partnering with an exciting and fast growing organisation seeking a Senior Data Engineer to join their Data function. This is an opportunity to gain Agentic and Generative AI "Productionised" experience. This role offers strong Career Development within an organisation which operates with very little red-tape and has a supportive and positive culture.

About the Role:

The Senior Data Engineer is a hands on technical specialist responsible for building and supporting modern Databricks Lakehouse solutions on Azure within a complex retail data landscape. Working across both project initiatives and BAU, the role delivers robust, scalable data pipelines and curated analytical datasets that underpin enterprise BI, advanced analytics, and AI. You will collaborate closely with retail stakeholders, BI developers, and broader technology teams to transform high volume retail data into trusted, production ready insights that support critical business outcomes. This is an opportunity to gain Agentic and Generative AI "Productionised" experience.

What You Will Do:

Platform & Analytics Enablement

Enable enterprise BI, analytics, and AI driven insights by delivering scalable, high‑quality data solutions that support retail trading, operational, and customer use cases.
Build and optimise data pipelines and integrations that underpin reporting, advanced analytics, and AI/ML workloads.
Ensure analytics and reporting solutions are performant, secure, scalable, and fit for enterprise and retail decision making.

Azure & Databricks Data Engineering

Design, develop, and evolve modern data engineering solutions on Microsoft Azure, with a strong focus on Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory (ADF), and Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS).
Develop robust data transformations using PySpark, Databricks SQL, SQL, and Python.
Design, implement, and maintain curated analytical and dimensional data models aligned to retail BI, analytics, and AI requirements.

BI & Semantic Layer Delivery

Develop, optimise, and support Power BI datasets, semantic models, and reports to deliver trusted, business ready insights.
Administer Power BI environments, including workspace management, refresh orchestration, security, and access controls.
Align BI solutions to best practice data modelling, performance optimisation, and usability standards.

Production Support & Operational Excellence

Provide BAU support across data platforms, pipelines, BI, and analytics solutions, including issue resolution and root cause analysis.
Monitor data platforms and pipelines, implementing logging, alerting, and performance tuning.
Proactively identify and resolve data quality, availability, and performance issues in production environments.

Engineering Standards, DevOps & Security

Implement and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data and analytics solutions using Azure DevOps.
Apply cloud security, authentication, and access control best practices across Azure data and analytics services.
Contribute to design reviews, code reviews, and technical discussions to ensure robust, maintainable, and scalable solutions.
Support the definition and continuous improvement of data engineering, analytics, and BI standards.

Governance & Collaboration

Contribute to the design, adoption, and communication of data governance practices across the data platform.
Partner closely with retail business stakeholders to translate requirements into reliable technical solutions.
Collaborate with engineering, data science, infrastructure, and DevOps teams to deliver secure, production ready solutions.
Balance delivery across project initiatives and ongoing BAU activities within a complex retail data environment.


What You Will Bring:


Strong hands on Data Engineering experience with SQL, Python, PySpark and Databricks SQL.
Strong experience designing and delivering data solutions on Microsoft Azure - Azure Databricks, Azure Data Factory (ADF), Azure Data Lake Storage (ADLS).
Proven experience designing and building data warehouses and analytical data models, including scalable lakehouse and dimensional modelling patterns.
Strong understanding of data integration, analytics platforms, and reporting architectures in enterprise environments.
Hands‑on experience administering and developing Power BI solutions, including Dataset and semantic model design, Strong DAX capability, Performance optimisation and governance.
Experience working in DevOps environments, including - CI/CD pipelines, Source control, Release and environment management.
General understanding of cloud authentication, security, and access control concepts, including role‑based access and data security principles.
Demonstrated ability to deliver both project work and BAU/support, operating confidently in a production data and BI environment.
Proven retail sector Data Engineering experience to Senior Data Engineer level.

Why Join:

Opportunity to gain Agentic and Generative AI "Productionised" experience.
Strong Career Development.
Amazing culture with very little red tape.

If you are a Senior Data Engineer with strong stakeholder management, who has experience in a fast-paced retail environment, and is outcome focussed we would love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out for a confidential conversation.

At the centre of the Wesfarmers Group, we are building a cross-skilled and diverse technology team focused on engaging the business, understanding strategic drivers, co-creating value-adding solutions and pushing technical boundaries to innovate and deliver excellence to the Corporate Office.

As Senior AI Transformation Analyst, you are accountable for identifying, analysing, and packaging AI transformation opportunities across the Wesfarmers Corporate Office. Working directly with business teams, you will develop a deep understanding of how each function operates, where friction and inefficiency exist, and how AI can meaningfully change the way work gets done.

You will translate business needs into well-defined AI opportunity briefs that the AI delivery team can act on, bridging the gap between business intent and technical execution. This requires equal comfort operating in business conversations and in the AI tool landscape, with the credibility to guide teams through what is possible and what is practical.

Working at the interface between the business and the AI delivery team, you will ensure that transformation opportunities are well understood, clearly defined, and ready to build. Once solutions are in place, you will support the business through the transition to new ways of working, helping teams build the confidence and capability needed to get lasting value from AI capabilities.

This is an 18 month max-term position based in Perth.

What You’ll Do

•                    Engage with Corporate Office functions to develop a thorough understanding of current processes, ways of working, pain points, and strategic priorities.

•                    Identify and assess opportunities where AI can deliver meaningful transformation, efficiency, or quality improvements across functions including Finance, Legal, HR, Corporate Affairs, and others.

•                    Conduct structured discovery sessions with function leads and team members to surface AI use cases, prioritise them by value and feasibility, and build a pipeline of transformation opportunities for delivery.

•                    Develop well-structured AI opportunity briefs and business requirements that give the Manager, AI Delivery and Senior AI Engineers the context they need to design and build effective solutions.

•                    Act as the primary liaison between the business function and the AI delivery team throughout the delivery lifecycle, providing clarity on requirements and facilitating decisions where needed.

•                    Develop change management plans for AI transformation initiatives, considering the people, process, and capability dimensions of each change.

•                    Maintain a current and practical understanding of the enterprise AI tool landscape, including Microsoft 365 Copilot, Claude, ChatGPT Enterprise, and Google Gemini, with the ability to explain their capabilities and appropriate use cases to non-technical audiences.

•                    Build trusted relationships with leaders and team members across Corporate Office functions, acting as a credible and practical advisor on AI transformation.

 

What You’ll Bring

•                    Demonstrated experience in a business analysis, process improvement, or transformation role within a complex organisation.

•                    Working knowledge of the enterprise AI tool landscape, along with practical exposure to AI tools and GenAI platforms in a professional context, with the ability to assess their applicability to business problems.

•                    Experience facilitating discovery workshops and engaging stakeholders at various levels to elicit and document requirements.

•                    Experience designing or supporting change management programs, including stakeholder engagement, communication planning, and adoption tracking.

•                    Exposure to working alongside technical delivery teams, with an understanding of how business requirements flow into design and build activities.

•                    Excellent written communication skills, with the ability to produce clear and well-structured briefing documents, opportunity assessments, and executive materials.

•                    Strong interpersonal and relationship-building skills, with the ability to build trust quickly across diverse teams.

•                    Tertiary qualified in relevant technology discipline (Solution Architecture, Business Improvement, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Mathematics, Computer Science).

•                    Lean or 6 Sigma equivalent certification preferred Work experience.

 

Technology used

•                    Microsoft 365 suite including Copilot.

•                    Enterprise GenAI tools (Claude, ChatGPT Enterprise, Google Gemini).

•                    Process mapping and diagramming tools (Visio, Lucidchart or equivalent).

•                    Process automation tools (UIPath, Kognitos or equivalent).

•                    Project and task management platforms.

 

About Wesfarmers

As one of Australia’s largest listed companies, we’re proud of our longstanding contribution to Australians.

We continually reinvent ourselves and invest in the businesses we own and operate, including leading retailers like Bunnings, Kmart, Target, Officeworks and Priceline. We also have an online offering, including OnePass and the Group shared data asset.

Our work doesn’t stop there, we operate in the industrials sector which supplies the nation with chemicals, energy and fertilisers, and industrial and safety products. And we participate in an integrated lithium joint venture, including the operation of a mine and concentrator and the development of a refinery.

Join the team at Corporate Office which provides support and advice across the Wesfarmers Group

The Corporate Office provides corporate services and support to the autonomous businesses within the Wesfarmers Group, enabling the achievement of Group objectives. It leads capital allocation and portfolio management for the Group and focuses on leveraging and enhancing the Group’s reputation to support attraction and retention of talent, securing investment opportunities and maintaining a low-cost capital.

To do this, we need great people, working together and role modelling our values and ways of working.

Represent Pinkerton’s core values of integrity, vigilance, and excellence.

Manage the Online Enforcement Program using the Corsearch AI-enabled e-commerce monitoring system to analyze and address trademark infringements.

Oversee investigations into potential IP violations, manage test buy programs to gather evidence of counterfeiting, and collaborate with Pinkerton's Global Investigations Unit for supply chain probes and enforcement actions.

Able to appropriately prioritize workload with competing deadlines.

Effective written and verbal communication skills.

Relationship management skills.

Able to collaborate with virtual teams to achieve common business goals.

Client orientated and results driven.

Able to maintain confidentiality and manage sensitive information.

Computer skills; Microsoft Office, Corsearch, AI-enabled analysis, and investigative software.
